question.text: A company issues a public debt offering with a base issue size of Rs. 150 crore. What is the maximum total amount the company can raise including oversubscription retention (without shelf prospectus)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Without a shelf prospectus, oversubscription up to 100% of the base issue size can be retained. So the maximum total = Base issue (Rs. 150 crore) + Oversubscription retention (100% of Rs. 150 crore = Rs. 150 crore) = Rs. 300 crore. **The Trap:** Rs. 225 crore assumes only 50% oversubscription retention, and Rs. 375 crore assumes 150% retention — neither is correct under SEBI ILDS norms. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX calculation questions often test the ability to apply percentage-based limits to base figures across different issue types. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Plan your issue structure so the base issue covers your minimum funding need, and the oversubscription retention gives flexibility — never assume you'll get the full 100% green shoe.
